data.title: The Daily: Vanguard explores offering access to crypto ETFs, regulators probe suspicious stock trades ahead of DAT purchases, and more
data.description:
The following article is adapted from The Block’s newsletter, The Daily, which comes out on weekday afternoons.

Happy Friday! Bitcoin briefly slipped below $109k into the U.S. PCE print before bouncing back after the data landed as expected. Still, the week's tone is defensive: spot ETFs saw outflows, leverage was flushed, and analysts say bulls need to reclaim the $113k–$116k zone with volume to flip the script.

In today's newsletter, Vanguard explores offering crypto ETF access to brokerage clients, regulators probe suspicious stock trades made ahead of crypto treasury announcements, SEC Chair Paul Atkins responds to crypto conflict of interest concerns, and more.

Meanwhile, Swift reportedly experiments with onchain migration using Ethereum Layer 2 Linea.

Let's get started!

P.S. Don't forget to check out The Funding, a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read — and just like The Daily, it's free to subscribe!

Vanguard explores offering access to crypto ETFs

Vanguard is reportedly weighing whether to let brokerage clients access third-party crypto ETFs, potentially reversing its long-held policy of rejecting digital asset products, a source told Crypto in America.

  • In January 2024, when the first spot bitcoin ETFs launched in the U.S., Vanguard famously stated that it wouldn't offer access to the new asset class, citing its high volatility as detrimental to generating long-term returns — a policy it had continued to reiterate since.
  • However, the $10 trillion asset management giant has faced growing client demand amid shifting regulatory winds under the Trump administration and new SEC leadership, the source said.
  • Timing and product choices are yet to be decided, the person added, confirming Vanguard still has no plans to launch a crypto ETF product of its own.
  • Bloomberg ETF Analyst Eric Balchunas called the move smart, noting the bitcoin and ether ETFs' huge popularity and Vanguard's reach across 50 million U.S. investors.
  • "We heard chatter of this too," he said. "Vanguard is looking to end bitcoin ETF ban (aka bend the knee lol)."

Regulators probe suspicious stock trades before crypto treasury announcements

The SEC and FINRA are investigating unusual stock trading activity before digital asset treasury firms announced plans to purchase cryptocurrencies, the WSJ reported, citing sources familiar with the matter.

  • Regulators flagged sharp price jumps and high trading volumes ahead of the announcements as potential red flags.
  • SEC officials reportedly cautioned some companies about possible Regulation Fair Disclosure violations tied to selective information sharing.
  • The probes come as crypto treasury strategies surge, with more than 200 DATs running the Michael Saylor-inspired playbook and raising over $20 billion so far this year.

SEC Chair Atkins responds to crypto conflict of interest concerns

During a Financial Markets and Policy conference at Georgetown University, SEC Chair Paul Atkins said the agency will enforce laws and investigate conflicts of interest in crypto "where necessary," declining to address specific companies or individuals.

  • His comments come amid scrutiny of the Trump family–linked DeFi project World Liberty Financial and its ties to UAE firm MGX, Binance, and the USD1 stablecoin, particularly from Democratic lawmakers.
  • Atkins has broken from predecessor Gary Gensler by pursuing a friendlier stance toward crypto, including an "innovation exemption" to fast-track onchain products by year-end.
  • He also launched a "Project Crypto" initiative to modernize SEC rules, clarify what qualifies as a security, and provide lawyers and firms greater regulatory certainty.

DeFi protocol Hypervault vanishes after $3.6 million suspected rugpull

Blockchain security firm PeckShield flagged $3.6 million in suspicious outflows from DeFi platform Hypervault, with funds bridged from Hyperliquid to Ethereum.
  • Around 752 ETH, worth nearly $3 million, was later funneled into crypto mixer Tornado Cash — a move often associated with rug pulls.
  • Hypervault's website also went offline, and its X account was deleted, adding to exit scam suspicions. The project had marketed its "unmanaged," auto-compounding vaults, and modular yield strategies before abruptly vanishing.

Korean actor handed suspended prison term for embezzling $3 million to invest in crypto

Famous South Korean actor Hwang Jung-eum received a two-year prison sentence, suspended for four years, after embezzling $3 million from her own management agency to invest in crypto.

  • Prosecutors said she diverted funds 13 times in 2022, using most for crypto and the rest for taxes and personal expenses.
  • The court gave leniency after Hwang fully repaid the money and cited her clean record as a first-time offender.

Looking ahead to next week

  • UK GDP data are out on Tuesday. Eurozone CPI figures follow on Wednesday. U.S. jobless claims numbers are due on Thursday. U.S. nonfarm payrolls follow on Friday.
  • ECB President Christine Lagarde will speak on Tuesday and Friday. Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey also speaks on Friday.
  • Artificial Superintelligence Alliance, Jupiter, Optimism, 1inch, Sui, dYdX, EigenLayer, and Ethena are among the crypto projects set for token unlocks.
  • TOKEN2049 kicks off in Singapore.

Never miss a beat with The Block's daily digest of the most influential events happening across the digital asset ecosystem.

Disclaimer: This article was produced with the assistance of OpenAI’s ChatGPT 3.5/4 and reviewed and edited by our editorial team.

Disclaimer: The Block is an independent media outlet that delivers news, research, and data. As of November 2023, Foresight Ventures is a majority investor of The Block. Foresight Ventures invests in other companies in the crypto space. Crypto exchange Bitget is an anchor LP for Foresight Ventures. The Block continues to operate independently to deliver objective, impactful, and timely information about the crypto industry. Here are our current financial disclosures.

© 2025 The Block. All Rights Reserved. This article is provided for informational purposes only. It is not offered or intended to be used as legal, tax, investment, financial, or other advice.

data.title: Ferguson Enterprises Inc. /DE/ SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Ferguson Enterprises Inc., the largest value-added distributor in the North American construction market specializing in water and air solutions, has released its 2025 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $30,762 million, an increase of 3.8% compared with 2024, primarily driven by higher sales volume and incremental sales from acquisitions.
  • Gross profit: $9,435 million, an increase of 4.2% compared with fiscal 2024, reflecting specific management actions to better capture the value provided to customers.
  • Operating profit: $2,606 million, a decrease of 1.7% compared to fiscal 2024, primarily due to $80 million in non-recurring restructuring expenses.
  • Net income: $1,856 million, an increase of 7.0% compared with fiscal 2024, driven by non-recurring, non-cash deferred tax charges incurred in fiscal 2024.
  • Earnings per share - Diluted: $9.32, increasing 9.3% compared with the prior year due to higher net income and the impact of share repurchases.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: Ferguson is the largest value-added distributor in the North American construction market, specializing in water and air solutions. The company offers a wide range of products and services, including plumbing, HVAC, appliances, lighting, PVF, and water and wastewater solutions.
  • Geographical Performance: The United States segment contributed 95% of net sales in fiscal years 2025, 2024, and 2023, operating under the Ferguson brand. The Canada segment, operating under the Wolseley brand, contributed 5% of net sales in the same periods.
  • Sales Units: As of July 31, 2025, Ferguson operated 1,519 branches and 10 regional distribution centers in the United States, and 227 branches in Canada, with a total workforce of approximately 35,000 associates.
  • Business Model: Ferguson serves both residential and non-residential markets, with a balanced approach to repair, maintenance, improvement, and new construction. The company operates in highly fragmented markets, leveraging its scale and expertise to bridge the gap between suppliers and customers.
  • Value-Added Solutions: Ferguson provides a variety of sales channels and customized solutions, including virtual design, fabrication, valve actuation, pre-assembly, kitting, installation, and project management services to enhance customer productivity.
  • Global Supply Chain: The company maintains a global supply chain with approximately 37,000 suppliers and sells over 1 million unique products annually. Its network includes 11 regional distribution centers and six market distribution centers with automated systems.
  • Competitive Conditions: Ferguson holds leading positions in its markets, focusing on growth through existing business expansion and strategic acquisitions. The company aims to leverage its scale and expertise to serve customers better than local competitors.
  • Human Capital Management: Ferguson emphasizes attracting, developing, and retaining top talent, offering various leadership and development programs. The company fosters a culture of integrity and ethical conduct, supported by its Code of Business Conduct and Ethics.
  • Future Outlook: Ferguson plans to continue its growth strategy by expanding its branch network and enhancing capabilities through acquisitions. The company anticipates strong growth opportunities and continued market consolidation.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Streamlining Operations: Ferguson implemented targeted actions to streamline operations, enhancing speed and efficiency to better serve customers and drive further profitable growth. This included $73 million in non-recurring business restructuring expenses.
  • Investments: The company invested $301 million in acquisitions and $305 million in capital expenditures to meet strategic objectives, focusing on new market distribution centers, branch network expansion, and new technology.
  • Capital Management: Ferguson repurchased $948 million of its own shares under the announced share repurchase program and paid $489 million in dividends to shareholders. The company also issued $750 million in Unsecured Senior Notes maturing in March 2031, bearing interest at 4.35%. Ferguson maintained a strong liquidity position with $674 million in cash and cash equivalents and $2.0 billion of available liquidity from undrawn debt facilities. Total debt as of July 31, 2025, was $4.2 billion.
  • Future Outlook: Ferguson anticipates that its current cash position, coupled with cash flow from operations and access to capital, will be sufficient to meet operating cash requirements for the next 12 months. The company plans to continue investing in strategic acquisitions and capital expenditures to support future growth. Additionally, Ferguson aims to maintain its focus on debt management and shareholder returns through dividends and share repurchases.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market Risks: Ferguson operates in highly fragmented markets with significant competition from both large and small distributors. The company is exposed to risks related to its global supply chain, which includes approximately 37,000 suppliers. Any disruption in this supply chain could adversely affect operations. Additionally, the company is subject to various domestic and international regulations, which could impact its business operations.
  • Operational Risks: Ferguson faces several market risks, including economic conditions, interest rate fluctuations, and competition. The company is particularly vulnerable to changes in the U.S. economy, where it generates 95% of its net sales. Operational risks include potential disruptions in the supply chain, cybersecurity threats, and the need to adapt to changing customer preferences.
  • Regulatory Risks: Regulatory risks involve compliance with evolving laws and standards, particularly in data protection and environmental regulations. New and emerging risks include the impact of geopolitical tensions and trade restrictions, which could increase costs and affect competitiveness. The company also faces challenges related to its indebtedness, which could limit strategic flexibility.
  • Management Strategies: Management has identified the need to enhance operational efficiency and customer service as key strategies to address competitive pressures. The company is investing in technology to improve supply chain management and customer engagement. Additionally, Ferguson is focusing on acquisitions to expand its market presence and capabilities.
  • Market Risk Disclosures: Ferguson is exposed to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, particularly with the Canadian dollar, which could impact financial results. The company also faces risks related to commodity price volatility, which can affect the cost of goods sold and operating margins. Interest rate changes could impact the cost of debt and overall financial performance.

SEC Filing: Ferguson Enterprises Inc. /DE/ [ FERG ] - 10-K - Sep. 26, 2025

data.title: Insurance startup Ethos Technologies files for US IPO
data.description:
Insurtech startup Ethos Technologies filed for an initial public offering in the United States on Friday.

The San Francisco, California-based company is the latest from the insurance industry looking to tap the new listings market. Nearly half a dozen of such companies have gone public in New York since May.

Ethos and some of its shareholders will sell shares in the offering.

Goldman Sachs and J.P. Morgan are the lead underwriters for the offering.

Ethos will list on the Nasdaq under the symbol "LIFE."

data.title: Specialty Beauty Retailers Continue to Outperform Drug, Department Stores — Market Talk
data.description: Speciality beauty and mass retailers continue to beat out drug and department stores in the beauty category, according to UBS in a note. The analysts say they found a sharp discrepancy in sales performance between players like Ulta, Sephora, Walmart and Amazon versus the likes of CVS, RiteAid, Walgreens, Macy's, Kohl's and Nordstrom. Those drug and department stores also continue to close stores, shutting down several hundred in the first half of the year, they say. That should allow room for further market share gains from differentiated players like Ulta going forward, they say. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

data.title: Soybeans Trade Tentatively as Chinese Absence Lingers — Market Talk
data.description: CBOT soybeans are up 0.1% as traders mull over what China's continued absence in buying U.S. grain exports mean for overall U.S. soybean export demand. "Soybeans remain in limbo as the market reconciles little export interest will likely remain for a few more weeks," says Rich Nelson of Allendale Inc., adding that Chinese demand could again return by December or January. Soybeans have been under pressure throughout September, shedding roughly 4% in that time. Most-active corn futures are down 0.7% in afternoon trade, while wheat falls 1.4%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: American Rebel: Financing Consolidated Under New Long-Term Agreement With Streeterville Capital >AREB
German online drug retailer Redcare Pharmacy CFO Eenhorst to step down
data.description:
German online drug retailer Redcare Pharmacy said on Friday that its CFO, Jasper Eenhorst, would step down at the end of the year.

CEO Olaf Heinrich will take over the responsibilities of Eenhorst in the interim, the company said.

data.title: Week Ahead - Sep 29th
data.description:
A busy week of major economic releases will be headlined by the US employment report, featuring fresh data for payrolls, unemployment, and wage growth that will impact the policy path for the Fed this year.

Other major labor market updates include the ADP employment report, results from the JOLTS, and Challenger job cuts.

Besides labor updates, investors will also be alert on the risk of a US government shutdown amid the start of the new fiscal year.

In Europe, fresh inflation data for the Eurozone and its members are due, in addition to CPI publications by Turkey and Switzerland.

Key leading indicators are also expected stateside in ISM PMIs, coinciding with PMI releases in China, Italy, Canada, Brazil, Mexico, South Korea, and ASEAN states.

On the monetary policy front, rate decisions are due in Australia and India

data.title: Boeing, IAM union to resume contract negotiations
data.description: The International Association of Machinists and Aerospace Workers and Boeing have agreed to return to negotiations on Monday with the help of a federal mediator in an effort to end a near two-month strike, the union said on Friday.

data.title: Rice Futures Hit Multi-Year Low
data.description:
Rice futures fell to $11.25 per hundredweight, reaching levels not seen since August 2019, as ample global supplies and a rapid US harvest pressured the market.

The bearish sentiment is largely fueled by forecasts of record-high global production and bumper crops from major exporters, particularly India, where rice stocks have exceeded 48 million metric tons following the government’s earlier decision to lift export restrictions.

US rice supplies are also elevated, with the USDA projecting record or near-record ending stocks, which has dampened the competitiveness of American exports.

Adding to the downward pressure, the US harvest is progressing quickly, with Arkansas farmers having already harvested 60% of planted acres.

data.description:
RNS Number : 0873B Legal & General Group Plc 26 September 2025  

Legal & General Group Plc

Notification of Transactions of Directors, Persons Discharging Managerial Responsibilities ("PDMRs") and persons closely associated with them

In line with the announcement made on 21 March 2025, as part of the remuneration terms relating to the appointment of Katie Worgan as Group Chief Operating Officer, Legal & General Group Plc ("the Company") agreed to compensate her for incentive awards forfeited by her on leaving her former employer.

In accordance with such remuneration terms, on 21 March 2025, the Company granted Katie Worgan restricted share awards of a total of 97,540 ordinary shares of 2.5p each (the "Shares") in the Company under the Share Bonus Plan (the "Plan"). Further to the announcement made on 21 March 2025, and following the Plan, the first tranche of Katie's restricted share awards of 13,773 shares vested on 23 September 2025.

The awards were granted on the basis of an average closing share price on the London Stock Exchange between 2nd and 4th September 2024 of £2.238. No consideration was paid for the grant of the awards. No price is payable on the vesting of the awards.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 1 PM ET: Inflation rises again, PCE shows, but not enough to alarm the Fed | Stocks ...
data.description:
Inflation rises again, PCE shows, but not enough to alarm the Fed

A key measure of inflation rose at a slightly elevated pace in August, but not enough to suggest much bigger increases tied to U.S. tariffs are on the way.

----

Stocks Rise After Trump Unveils New Tariffs

U.S. stocks were mostly higher, putting the Dow on track to break a three-day losing streak after President Trump unveiled new tariffs on drugs, trucks and furniture.

----

Trump to Impose New Tariffs on Pharma, Big Trucks

Levies on drugs will be imposed on companies that aren't building plants in the U.S.

----

Consumer Sentiment Fell in September, According to University of Michigan Survey

The monthly survey's sentiment index fell to 55.1 in September, from 58.2 in August amid worries about high prices and the labor market.

----

Canada Economy Stalls in August After First Growth in Four Months

Canada's economy is on track to eke out tepid growth for the latest quarter, avoiding a second consecutive contraction.

----

Week Ahead for FX, Bonds: U.S. Jobs Data in Focus

Investors will hone in on U.S. jobs data, where any signs of weakness could reignite prospects of a series of interest-rate cuts in the months to come.

----

Fed's Barkin upbeat on U.S. economic outlook despite rising inflation, slower job growth

"The consumer is frankly exhausted," Tom Barkin said Friday.

----

Tokyo's Price Growth Steady But Rate Increase Still in Play

The pace of price growth in the Tokyo metropolitan area unexpectedly held steady in September, but upward pressure remains, keeping an interest-rate hike in play.

----

Europe's Right-Wing Populists Swerve Left on the Economy

The adoption of a high-spending, pro-welfare and protectionist agenda has been key in winning over blue-collar workers.

----

Fed Independence Reaches Its Moment of Truth as Supreme Court Weighs Cook's Fate

The court is poised to decide whether President Trump can remove Lisa Cook from the Federal Reserve's board, which former officials see as threat to central bank independence.

----

Democrats Dig In on Shutdown Stance After White House Threatens to Fire Workers

Republicans want a short-term deal to keep the government open, while Democratic leaders seek healthcare funds.

----

Bank of Mexico Makes 10th Straight Interest-Rate Cut

The board of governors voted 4-1 to lower the overnight interest-rate target to 7.50% from 7.75%.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Company Headlines at 1 PM ET: Boeing Clears Key Hurdle From 737 MAX Crashes | AstraZeneca ...
data.description:
Boeing Clears Key Hurdle From 737 MAX Crashes

The aerospace giant is regaining some authority from regulators to perform final safety checks on jets.

----

AstraZeneca to Offer Discounted Drugs As Trump Pressures Pharma Industry to Cut Prices

AstraZeneca said it will offer its asthma and diabetes drugs at an up to 70% discount in the U.S. ahead of a Trump administration deadline for pharmaceutical companies to cut drug prices.

----

Banks Ordered to Dig Through Account Closures to Find 'Debanking' Cases

Regulators demand information going back years as part of President Trump's crackdown on alleged political or religious discrimination.

----

Amgen to Invest $650 Million in U.S. Production Expansion as Trump Hikes Tariffs

The investment was announced a day after President Trump said he would impose a 100% tariff on pharmaceuticals produced by companies that are not building plants in the U.S.

----

Trump to Impose New Tariffs on Pharma, Big Trucks

Levies on drugs will be imposed on companies that aren't building plants in the U.S.

----

Meta to Launch Ad-Free Service on Facebook, Instagram in U.K.

The move matches an ad-free service launched in the EU in 2023, but hasn't been rolled out to the U.S. or other parts of the world.

----

U.S. Takes Aim at Chip Makers With New Plan to Throttle Imports

The administration wants domestic manufacturing to match imports and would impose tariffs on those companies that don't step up production.

----

Six Flags Is Under Activist Pressure to Sell Its Real Estate

Land & Buildings Investment Management has about a 2% stake in the theme-park operator.

----

How Zillow Got On Track for First Profitable Year Since 2012

The real-estate listings company has focused on boosting revenue and slowing head count growth to reach its profitability goal, CFO says.

----

GTCR to Buy Dentalcorp in Deal Valuing Dental Network at About $1.58 Billion

Dentalcorp said its shareholders will receive C$11 a share in cash, a nearly 33% premium to Thursday's closing price of C$8.28 for the Toronto company.

----

Brunello Cucinelli Shares Continue Slide Following Russia Claims

The company needs to start damage limitation as soon as possible to protect its reputation with customers and investors alike, Bernstein said.

----

Why Meta Thinks It Can Challenge Apple in Consumer AI Devices

Meta is betting that the future of artificial intelligence takes users way beyond smartphones and personal computers.

----

Intel Seeks Help From Apple and Others as U.S. Backs Comeback Bid

The semiconductor maker has been winning support from other tech companies following the deal that gave the U.S. government a 10% stake.

----

Nvidia's AI Deals Might Be 'Circular.' They're Also Smart Business.

Nvidia's cash pile is growing. It's using the money to backstop the future of AI.

data.title: Alaska Air Group Announces Leadership Transitions at Alaska Airlines and Horizon Air
data.description:
• Jason Berry to be named Chief Operating Officer of Alaska Airlines, succeeding Constance von Muehlen who will retire after a ground-breaking career in aviation
  • Alaska Air Group veteran, Andy Schneider, to be appointed CEO and President of Horizon Air

SEATTLE, Sept. 26, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Alaska Air Group today announced several leadership transitions that reflect the organization's commitment to operational excellence and its people-first culture.

After nearly 15 years of dedicated service to Alaska Airlines, Chief Operating Officer, Constance von Muehlen will retire. Von Muehlen joined Alaska in 2011 and became COO in 2021, where she led the airline through a period of significant growth and transformation. Her leadership has been instrumental in advancing safety, reliability, and innovation across the airline's operations.

Succeeding von Muehlen as Chief Operating Officer will be Jason Berry, a 30-year industry veteran who currently serves as President of Horizon Air and Executive Vice President of Cargo for Alaska Air Group. Berry brings deep operational expertise and a strong track record of leading teams with heart and precision. His experience spans cargo, ground services, and regional operations, including leadership roles at Air Canada, Cargolux and McGee Air Services. Berry's role will be effective November 3, 2025, with von Muehlen staying on until February 2026 in an advisory capacity to support a seamless transition.

With Berry stepping into the COO role at Alaska Airlines, Andy Schneider will be named CEO and President of Horizon Air. Schneider currently serves as Senior Vice President of People at Alaska Airlines and has played a pivotal role in shaping the company's culture and talent strategy. Over her more than 35 years with Air Group, Schneider has held various operations roles including as Alaska's vice president of inflight and call center services and senior vice president of people and customer services at Horizon. Her leadership will guide Horizon Air into its next chapter, continuing its legacy of exceptional regional service.

Over the last year, Alaska Airlines and Hawaiian Airlines teams have worked together to build and execute the plan to become a single mainline operating carrier under a single operating certificate (SOC) from the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), which is anticipated later this fall.

"These transitions mark an exciting moment for Alaska Air Group, and the milestone of a single operating certificate is a natural time to evolve our leadership team as we grow into a global premium airline," said Ben Minicucci, CEO of Alaska Air Group. "I am deeply grateful for Constance's remarkable contributions during an exceptionally dynamic time for our company and industry, and I am thrilled to welcome Jason and Andy into their new roles. Their leadership will help us continue to deliver on our commitment to connect guests to the world through a remarkable experience rooted in safety, care and performance."

"It has been an absolute privilege to lead Alaska's frontline teams these past 5 years - out of the pandemic, into an integration and always putting safety and excellence at the forefront," said von Muehlen. "Every day, I get to see how Alaska regularly leads the industry in care and performance, and it's because of the dedication of thousands of technicians, flight attendants, pilots, customer service agents, dispatchers and everyone in between. I know they will continue this excellence with Jason at the helm."

"This is an incredibly exciting time at Alaska and Hawaiian Airlines. As we move toward the next integration milestone, a single operating certificate, we're bringing together incredible frontline teams who will continue to lead the industry in safety, remarkable customer service and operational excellence," Berry said. "I am so honored to be taking on this new role as we build on the great foundation Constance and her team have established and know that Horizon is in great hands with Andy as CEO and President."

"I am so humbled and honored to be returning to Horizon after more than 14 years," said Schneider. "What makes Horizon special are the incredible people who work here and the important role they play as a lifeline for the communities we serve. I'm so excited for this next chapter as we build on the rich legacy the Horizon team has established."

For more information, visit news.alaskaair.com.

About Alaska Air Group

Alaska Airlines, Hawaiian Airlines and Horizon Air are subsidiaries of Alaska Air Group, and McGee Air Services is a subsidiary of Alaska Airlines. We are a global airline with hubs in Seattle, Honolulu, Portland, Anchorage, Los Angeles, San Diego and San Francisco. We deliver remarkable care as we fly our guests to more than 140 destinations throughout North America, Latin America, Asia and the Pacific. We'll serve Europe beginning in spring 2026. Guests can book travel at alaskaair.com and hawaiianairlines.com. Alaska is a member of the oneworld alliance, with Hawaiian scheduled to join oneworld in spring 2026. With oneworld and our additional global partners, guests can earn and redeem points for travel to over 1,000 worldwide destinations with Atmos Rewards. Learn more about what's happening at Alaska and Hawaiian at news.alaskaair.com. Alaska Air Group is traded on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) as "ALK."

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/alaska-air-group-announces-leadership-transitions-at-alaska-airlines-and-horizon-air-302568389.html

SOURCE Alaska Airlines

Change in Redcare Pharmacy’s Managing Board: CFO Jasper Eenhorst to step down.
data.description:
EQS-News: Redcare Pharmacy N.V./ Key word(s): Personnel

Change in Redcare Pharmacy’s Managing Board: CFO Jasper Eenhorst to step down.

26.09.2025 / 19:05 CET/CEST

The issuer is solely responsible for the content of this announcement.

Change in Redcare Pharmacy’s Managing Board: CFO Jasper Eenhorst to step down.

Sevenum, the Netherlands, 26 September 2025. After almost six years as CFO, Jasper Eenhorst will leave Redcare Pharmacy to take on new career opportunities. He is stepping down from the Managing Board today, but will remain with the company until the end of this year to support a smooth transition to his successor. 

Björn Söder, Chairman of the Supervisory Board comments: “On behalf of the entire Supervisory Board, I would like to sincerely thank Jasper for his exceptional commitment and valuable contribution to the company’s successful development and growth during his tenure. Jasper was instrumental in several successful capital market transactions, among others, the placement of €300 million senior unsecured convertible bonds last April which extended the company’s debt maturity and laid a solid basis for further growth. We have developed our business model over the past years and expanded our market leadership - now also in the German prescription market. We have turned Redcare Pharmacy into a profitable growth story ready for the future. This is a natural moment for Jasper to move on. We wish him every success in his next chapters.”

The Supervisory Board has already initiated the search for a new CFO and expects it to be concluded soon. Until a successor has been appointed, the CFO’s responsibilities will be assumed on an interim basis by Olaf Heinrich, CEO of Redcare Pharmacy. 

Jasper Eenhorst states: “It has been a privilege to serve Redcare Pharmacy as CFO during a period of significant growth and transformation. I am grateful for the trust placed in me and for the years I have spent as part of the team. It was truly gratifying, rewarding and memorable, full of exceptional highlights. It is now the right moment for me to take the next step. I will ensure a smooth handover in the coming months and I am confident that the company will continue to thrive and achieve even greater success.” 

___

Investor Relations Contact:

Monica Ambrosi (Associate Director, Investor Relations)

investors@redcare-pharmacy.com

 

Press Contact:

Sven Schirmer (Director, Corporate Communications)

press@redcare-pharmacy.com 

About Redcare Pharmacy.

Originally founded in 2001, Redcare Pharmacy N.V. (formerly known as Shop Apotheke Europe N.V.) today is the leading e-pharmacy in Europe, currently active in seven countries: Germany, Austria, France, Belgium, Italy, the Netherlands and Switzerland.

Headquartered in Sevenum, close to the Dutch city of Venlo and in the heart of Europe, the company has locations in Cologne, Berlin, Munich, Tongeren, Warsaw, Milan, Lille and Eindhoven.

As the one-stop pharmacy of the future, Redcare Pharmacy offers over 13 million active customers a wide range of more than 250,000 products at attractive and fair prices. Besides OTC, nutritional supplements, beauty and personal care products as well as an extensive assortment of health-related products in all markets, the company also provides prescription drugs for customers in Germany, Switzerland and the Netherlands.

Pharmaceutical safety is of top priority. Being a pharmacy at its core, Redcare stands for comprehensive pharmaceutical consultation service. Since care is at the heart of everything Redcare does, the company provides services for all stages of life and health. This ranges from its marketplaces to unique delivery options and medication management.

Redcare Pharmacy N.V. has been listed on the Regulated Market of the Frankfurt Stock Exchange (Prime Standard) since 2016. As of 19 June 2023, the company is a member of the MDAX selection index.

26.09.2025 CET/CEST Dissemination of a Corporate News, transmitted by EQS News - a service of EQS Group.

The issuer is solely responsible for the content of this announcement.

The EQS Distribution Services include Regulatory Announcements, Financial/Corporate News and Press Releases.

data.title: Global Banking Giant SWIFT Ignites Mainstream Adoption With Ethereum Stablecoin Payment Test
data.description:
SWIFT, the backbone of global financial messaging, has begun testing on-chain payments and messaging using Ethereum’s Layer 2 network Linea, in a move that signals deeper integration between traditional finance and blockchain.

to the report, the project involves more than a dozen global banks, including BNP Paribas and BNY Mellon, and is exploring the use of a stablecoin-like token for settlement.

🟥 Exclusive SWIFT chooses Linea for blockchain testingAccording to information gathered with , SWIFT and several major global banks (including BNP Paribas and BNY) have chosen , the Ethereum layer 2 developed by , to experiment… — Grégory Raymond 🐳 (@gregory_raymond)

The trial marks a significant step for SWIFT, which connects over 11,000 financial institutions and processes billions of messages annually.

Traditionally, SWIFT has functioned as a secure messaging service that transmits payment instructions between banks. SWIFT Blockchain Pilot With Ethereum Could Redefine International Payments

Initial tests seek to focus on on-chain messaging and settlement functions, with the interbank stablecoin token serving as a model for how financial institutions could settle transactions directly on blockchain infrastructure.

SWIFT has emphasized that this effort goes beyond transmitting digital cash transfers, expanding its role into comprehensive on-chain activities.

The blockchain experiment seeks to extend this role into direct value transfer, potentially reducing reliance on multiple intermediaries and streamlining international settlements.

Notably, Linea was selected for its zk-rollup technology, which provides low-cost, high-throughput transactions while retaining Ethereum’s security. Its design also emphasizes data privacy through advanced cryptographic proofs, a feature seen as essential for banks navigating strict compliance requirements.

The experiment could take several months to materialize, but industry participants say it represents a major technological step forward for the interbank sector.

A banking source described it as a “significant transformation” for international payments, which today remain dependent on intermediaries and cumbersome legacy infrastructure.

The collaboration with Linea also builds on SWIFT’s earlier experiments in blockchain interoperability. In recent years, the network partnered with Chainlink to test cross-chain communication solutions.

🆕 Interbank messaging system SWIFT has partnered with price oracle provider () to work on a proof-of-concept (POC) project which would allow traditional finance firms to transact across blockchain networks.— Cryptonews.com (@cryptonews)

In August 2023, SWIFT the results of a series of trials examining how tokenized value can be transferred across both public and private blockchains.

At the time, the organization said the findings could help ease barriers that have slowed the expansion of tokenized asset markets, creating conditions for them to scale more efficiently on a global level as they continue to mature.

For Ethereum, the project reinforces its central role in financial experimentation.

Linea, launched by Consensys, is designed to scale Ethereum through zk-rollups, and its ecosystem is supported by the LINEA token, which rewards ETH stakers bridged onto the network.

By leveraging Ethereum’s security while introducing cost-efficient settlement, the Layer 2 platform has positioned itself as a bridge between traditional finance and decentralized systems.Banks and Big Tech Circle Stablecoin as Adoption Accelerates

Stablecoins are moving from niche crypto instruments into the mainstream of global finance, with adoption accelerating across technology, payments, and banking.

The market now exceeds $230 billion in value, led by Tether (USDT) and Circle (USDC), according to Morningstar DBRS.

💵 Morningstar warns stablecoins could drain U.S. bank deposits & payment fees. $230B+ market now dominated by USDT & USDC. — Cryptonews.com (@cryptonews)

Their rise has been fueled by speed and cost advantages: transactions settle instantly at minimal fees, compared with up to $50 and multi-day delays on traditional rails like SWIFT or wire transfers. Monthly volumes are surging.

Chainalysis data shows USDT clearing over $1 trillion per month this year, while USDC peaked at more than $3 trillion in activity last October.

Regulation is also reshaping the sector. Following the U.S. passage of its first federal stablecoin law in July, banks are weighing launches of their own tokens.

Analysts warn that widespread use could divert deposits and payment revenues away from traditional banks, with the Bank for International Settlements noting stablecoins already account for 1.5% of U.S. deposits.

Big tech is taking notice. Apple, Airbnb, Uber, and X have all held early talks on stablecoin integration, while Google Cloud has already accepted PYUSD payments.

On September 16, Google announced a new AI-focused payment framework supporting stablecoins alongside card networks, developed with Coinbase and the Ethereum Foundation.

🤖 Google announced stablecoin support for its new AI payment framework, partnering with Coinbase and Ethereum Foundation to enable seamless AI agent transactions. — Cryptonews.com (@cryptonews)

Institutional infrastructure is expanding in parallel. Fireblocks, a crypto services firm valued at $8 billion, has launched a stablecoin payments network with more than 40 participants, including Circle and Stripe-owned Bridge.

The system supports multiple stablecoins and is designed for enterprise cross-border transactions, with pilots underway in Japan.

Together, these moves point to a financial system where dollar-pegged digital tokens operate alongside, and potentially in competition with, legacy banking rails.

data.title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang Says OpenAI Will Be the Next 'Multi-Trillion-Dollar' Company — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Tae Kim

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang is bullish on OpenAI's prospects.

"I think that OpenAI is likely going to be the next multi-trillion-dollar hyperscale company," he said on the BG2 podcast published Thursday. "Hyperscale like Meta is hyperscale. Google is hyperscale. They're going to have consumer and enterprise services."

Meta Platforms and Alphabet, the parent company of Google, have market values of $1.9 trillion and $3 trillion, respectively.

On Monday, ChatGPT parent OpenAI and Nvidia announced a new partnership where the leading maker of AI chips plans to invest as much as $100 billion in OpenAI to support the buildout of 10 gigawatts of AI data center capacity. For context, one nuclear reactor can typically generate one gigawatt of electricity. OpenAI said ChatGPT, its main AI product, now has over 700 million weekly active users. That figure is up from 300 million in December.

Huang said if OpenAI is going to be that successful, the opportunity to invest in OpenAI will likely be one of the "smartest" investments to make now for Nvidia, noting the start-up is the "fastest-growing revenue company" in history.

"You ought to invest in things you know. It turns out we happen to know the space," he said. "The return on that money is going to be fantastic. We love the opportunity to invest. We don't have to invest."

The Nvidia CEO also played down the probability of a AI computing "glut" within the next five years. "Until we fully convert all general purpose computing [CPUs] to accelerated computing [GPUs] and AI, until we do that, I think the chances are extremely low," the executive said. "That will take a few years."

Huang is referencing the computing platform shift from the legacy model that used CPUs to retrieve static files to an AI-based GPU computing system that is able to distill and generate knowledge intelligence from vast pools of data.

Write to Tae Kim at tae.kim@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Ethos Technologies Files for IPO, Applied to List on the Nasdaq Global Select Market Under the Symbol LIFE
DATASEA INC. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Datasea Inc., a leading innovator in acoustic technology and AI multimodal digitalization, has released its annual Form 10-K report, showcasing substantial financial growth, strategic business expansions, and a clear focus on future technological advancements. The report highlights the company's impressive revenue increase, operational achievements, and strategic initiatives aimed at solidifying its market position both domestically and internationally.

Financial Highlights

Revenues: Datasea Inc. reported revenues of $71.62 million, marking an impressive increase of $47.64 million or 198.7% compared to the previous year. This growth was primarily driven by the rapid expansion of the 5G AI multimodal digital business in China.

Gross Profit: The company achieved a gross profit of $2.44 million, an increase of $1.97 million or 415.49% from the previous year. The gross margin improved to 3.4% from 2.0%, attributed to high-margin customized solution projects and increased market share.

Loss from Operations: Datasea reported a loss from operations of $(5.15) million, a decrease in loss by $6.97 million or 58.4% compared to the previous year. This improvement was due to increased gross profit and decreased operating expenses.

Net Loss to the Company: The net loss was $(5.09) million, a reduction in net loss by $6.29 million or 55.3% from the previous year, reflecting improved financial performance and cost management.

Business Highlights

Acoustic High-Tech Segment: Datasea focuses on the innovation of acoustic technology, particularly in non-hearable mechanical wave effects. Applications include environmental purification, health management, and industrial precision processing. The company has launched products like ultrasonic air purifiers and non-contact ultrasonic skin repair devices.

AI Multimodal Digitalization Segment: The company invests in AI multimodal digital technology, integrating 5G networks with AI and big data to create a comprehensive platform. This platform supports industries such as healthcare, logistics, and rural revitalization, offering services like intelligent marketing and business process automation.

Geographical Performance: Datasea operates primarily in China, with expanding operations in the United States through its subsidiary, Datasea Acoustics LLC. The company is promoting acoustic products and patent deployment in the U.S. market.

Sales and Distribution: The company has established a sales network in China, covering 463 beauty and health stores, and utilizes online platforms like Douyin and Xiaohongshu for live-streaming e-commerce. In the U.S., distribution partnerships with companies like iPower Inc. and Meglio Interiors LLC are in place.

New Product Launches: Datasea has introduced several new products, including ultrasonic disinfection devices, Schumann frequency sleep monitors, and ultrasonic skin repair devices. These products are designed for various settings, such as hospitals, airports, and residential areas.

Future Outlook: The company aims to become a global leader in acoustic technology and AI-driven enterprises, focusing on expanding the application of acoustic technologies across industries. Datasea plans to enhance its product offerings and market reach through continuous R&D and strategic partnerships.

Strategic Initiatives

Datasea Inc. is focusing on expanding its 5G AI multimodal digital business and acoustic intelligence business, with continuous product innovation and development. The company is also pursuing international market expansion, particularly in the U.S. and Europe, through partnerships and localized product introductions. Additionally, Datasea is actively seeking M&A opportunities to enhance its technological capabilities and market presence, particularly in the acoustics and AI sectors.

Capital Management: The company has been funding its operations primarily through the sale of common stock and shareholder loans. In October 2024, Datasea entered into subscription agreements with non-U.S. investors, raising approximately $4.0 million to support investments in acoustic high-tech products, working capital for mass production, and the promotion of AI multimodal digital business products. The company also secured several bank loans to enhance financial liquidity and support operational needs.

Future Outlook: Datasea plans to continue increasing R&D investment, particularly in the integration of acoustic technologies with AI, and to accelerate market expansion both domestically and internationally. The company aims to optimize its product mix by increasing the proportion of high-margin offerings and transitioning its acoustic high-tech business to integrated 'hardware + AI platform' solutions. Datasea is also focused on achieving high-quality growth and enhancing profitability through customized solutions and high-value-added services.

Challenges and Risks

The company faces significant risks related to its limited operating history in the highly competitive fields of acoustics high-tech and AI multimodal digital products. Despite revenue growth, the company continues to incur recurring losses, raising concerns about its ability to sustain operations without additional capital. The auditors have expressed substantial doubt about the company's ability to continue as a going concern due to its financial condition, including a net loss of $5.09 million in fiscal year 2025 and an accumulated deficit of $44.53 million.

The company anticipates needing additional capital to fund future growth, which may involve incurring debt or issuing new equity, potentially diluting existing shareholders. The ability to secure additional funding is uncertain and subject to various factors, including investor perceptions and regulatory conditions in China and the U.S.

Operational risks include reliance on third-party contract manufacturers, which could lead to increased costs or delays in fulfilling orders. Supply chain issues, such as financial problems of manufacturers or component suppliers, could adversely impact the company's business.

The company is also exposed to risks related to rapid technological changes and evolving industry standards, which require continuous investment in R&D and adaptation to maintain competitiveness. Failure to anticipate these changes could result in obsolete inventory and reduced profitability.

Regulatory risks are significant, particularly concerning the company's corporate structure involving Variable Interest Entities (VIEs) in China. Changes in PRC laws or their interpretation could force the company to alter its structure, potentially losing control over its operations and affecting its financial results.

Management acknowledges the challenges posed by the company's financial condition and the need for additional capital. Strategies to address these challenges include focusing on cost management and investing in key growth areas such as acoustics high-tech and AI multimodal digital products. However, the return on these investments may be lower or slower than expected, impacting operating results.

The company is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates, which could adversely affect financial results. Additionally, international tariffs, particularly between the U.S. and China, could increase costs and impact supply chain stability, affecting profitability and operational efficiency.

SEC Filing: DATASEA INC. [ DTSS ] - 10-K - Sep. 26, 2025

data.title: Ethos Technologies files for US IPO
data.description: Insurance firm Ethos Technologies on Friday filed for an initial public offering in the United States.
